### 1. **Living Room Lighting Function**

We are given the function for natural light entering the living room:

$$
N(t) = 300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

This function represents the natural light in lumens at time $ t $ (hours after sunrise). The LED light output must be adjusted so that the total illumination is always 500 lumens. Let $ L(t) $ be the LED light output in lumens.

The total illumination is the sum of the natural light and the LED light:

$$
\text{Total Illumination} = N(t) + L(t)
$$

We want this total to be constant at 500 lumens:

$$
N(t) + L(t) = 500
$$

Solving for $ L(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - N(t)
$$

Substitute the expression for $ N(t) $:

$$
L(t) = 500 - \left(300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right)
$$

$$
L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)
$$

### **Total Energy Consumption Over 24 Hours**

The LED consumes 0.1 watts per lumen-hour. The total energy consumption is:

$$
\text{Energy} = \int_0^{24} L(t) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

Substitute $ L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) $:

$$
\text{Energy} = \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \cdot 0.1 \, dt
$$

$$
= 0.1 \int_0^{24} \left(200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)\right) \, dt
$$
